title: Mathematical discoveries from program search with large language models
registry_note: Non-arXiv paper: included as a single terminal node because the result is load-bearing for the AI-for-math thread of this DAG. No LaTeX source available, so no structural extraction; the single node represents the headline numerical contribution. See SUMMARY.md for narrative context and README for the registry-wide convention on admitting non-arXiv nodes.

thm:funsearch-capset.summary: FunSearch — an LLM-guided evolutionary program-search system — discovers a new cap set of size $512$ in $\mathbb{F}_3^8$, exceeding the previous best of $496$ (the largest known explicit cap set in dimension $8$ for over $15$ years). The construction operates within Tyrrell's admissible-set framework: the LLM proposes Python scoring functions for candidate admissible sets, an evaluator scores them, and high-scoring candidates feed back into the LLM prompt. The improved admissible sets push the asymptotic cap-set lower-bound rate above the Tyrrell $2.218^n$ bound in specific dimensions, and demonstrate that LLM-guided search can discover combinatorial constructions that resist SAT-solver search. One of the coauthors (J. S. Ellenberg) is also the author of the cap-set upper bound (`1605.09223`).

ai_discovered_construction.description: Single load-bearing node: the headline numerical improvement (a 512-element cap set in $\mathbb{F}_3^8$, asymptotic lower-bound improvement). The full system description and ablations live in the Nature paper; only the mathematical result is represented here.
